source.title: Common Misunderstandings of the Trinity
source.summary: The Trinity is NOT: (1) Three separate gods (tritheism); (2) One God appearing in three different modes or masks (modalism); (3) 1+1+1=3 gods; (4) God, Jesus, and Mary; (5) God divided into three parts; (6) A later invention by the church. Rather, it affirms that the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit are distinct persons who share the one divine nature eternally and equally. Each is fully God, not partially God. This doctrine emerges from Scripture's witness to one God who exists as Father, Son, and Spirit.
